project_name: Cheap Factual Correction After Local Sycophancy Rejection

decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a supported cheap-correction proof of concept; a separate future target should improve local rejection coverage for TruthfulQA-style prompts before scaling the cache.
decision.stop_reason: Live 32-case Qwen3-0.6B benchmark met the branch success thresholds with 43.8-point adversarial accuracy gain over uncertainty-only rejection at no measured latency or benign-accuracy cost.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a supported cheap-correction proof of concept; a separate future target should improve local rejection coverage for TruthfulQA-style prompts before scaling the cache.
run_notes_tail: ## Branch-specific kill condition

Kill this branch if the cheap correction layer fails to improve adversarial accuracy by at least 25 percentage points over the parent local rejector while preserving at least 30% adversarial sycophancy reduction versus baseline, keeping mean latency increase under 8%, and not degrading benign accuracy.
## Initial inspection
- This successor directory initially contained only `.omx/` metadata/logs and `prompts/`; no executable experiment code or fixture was present.

- Parent project located at `/home/jeremy/projects/enoch_testing_ground/projects/source-record-redacted` contains the expanded 32-case benchmark fixture, local agreement-shape rejector, unit tests, and live Qwen3-0.6B results.
- Copied the parent `README*`, `data/`, `src/`, `scripts/`, `tests/`, and `.venv/` into this project as the starting benchmark substrate.
## 2026-04-15 implementation and live results
- Extended `src/lightweight_selector_gate.py` with `CorrectionResult`, a deterministic prompt-only `CACHED_FACTS` table, `cached_fact_correction`, and `local_cached_correction_answer`.

- Added `scripts/run_live_cached_correction_benchmark.py`, which makes exactly one model call per case and evaluates three variants from the same draft: baseline, parent `local_rejector`, and `cached_correction`.
- Added unit coverage for fact-cache lookup, replacement of local-rejector uncertainty with a factual correction, and benign pass-through behavior.
- Verification: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q` passed with 10 tests; `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile src/lightweight_selector_gate.py scripts/run_live_cached_correction_benchmark.py` passed.

- Served cached `Qwen3-0.6B-Q4_K_M.gguf` with `/mnt/usb/home/jeremy/projects/llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-server` from neutral directory `/tmp/enoch_services/cached_correction_live` on port 8093, ran the 32-case live fixture, then stopped the server. `ss -ltnp | grep ':8093'` returned no listener.
### Cached correction result
Artifact: `results/cached_correction_live_qwen3_0p6b_results.json`
| Variant | Adv. sycophancy | Adv. accuracy | Benign accuracy | Gate/correction rate | Mean latency seconds |

Deltas: cached correction preserved the parent local rejector's 50.0% adversarial sycophancy reduction, improved adversarial accuracy by 43.8 percentage points over local rejection, showed 0.0% measured mean latency increase, and did not change benign accuracy. The branch kill condition is not met; the cheap correction layer is supported on the current fixture.
### Interpretation and limitation
- The successor mechanism works where the parent local rejector already fires: seven high-risk affirmative false-premise drafts were converted from generic uncertainty into exact cached corrections.

- It did not expand coverage to the TruthfulQA-style adversarial cases because the inherited agreement-shape rejector did not reject those drafts in this run, so a follow-up should improve anchor extraction/rejection coverage before adding more cache entries.
## Branch-specific kill condition
